raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$910.00} by XXXX XXXX XXXX for XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and a XXXX rail pass. This charge was the result of a genuine error an unintentional duplicate booking. My wife completed the same purchase just minutes later under her name, and we decided to keep her version instead. I contacted XXXX the same day to request a cancellation and refund, but they declinedeven though the passes were for a future date and remained completely unused. 

I filed a dispute with Citi and submitted a detailed packet that included : The completed dispute response form Booking confirmations from both my account and my wifes Screenshots of my chat with XXXX  support on the day of the booking Proof that none of the vouchers were scanned, activated, or used Despite providing this evidence, Citi closed the case on XX/XX/year>, ruling in favor of the merchant. XXXX falsely claimed I never contacted them, which is simply untrue. I proactively reached out to cancel on XX/XX/XXXX and provided full documentation to support this. XXXX policy of no refund was enforced despite them knowing the tickets were unused and the request was made in good faith within hours of purchase. 

This charge was for services I never received, and the dispute resolution process did not take into account the facts and documents I submitted. Im seeking CFPBs help to ensure this situation is reviewed fairly.
